---
title: "Notes on the qQTL Manuscript Pipelines (Liu et al., 2026+)"
author: "qQTLR developers"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Notes on the qQTL Manuscript Pipelines (Liu et al., 2026+)}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  eval = FALSE
)
```

## Scope

The `qQTLR` R package provides **interactive, function-level** building
blocks for quantile QTL analysis: QRank-based screening, full quantile
regressions, vQTL detection via QUAIL, Chatterjee ξ heterogeneity
testing, and qTWAS weight training. For **end-to-end, production-scale
pipelines** — the exact workflows used to produce the results in the
qQTL manuscript (Liu et al., 2026+, FunGen-xQTL) — please refer to the
FunGen-xQTL protocol documentation listed below.

The manuscript-scale analyses combine `qQTLR` with upstream QC,
covariate modelling, multiple-testing machinery, enrichment
computations, and downstream association testing that are orchestrated
outside the R package itself.

## Pipeline references

### qQTL calling

End-to-end per-gene qQTL scan (QRank screen, full quantile regressions,
quantile-specific effect sizes), producing the tables consumed
downstream:

<https://statfungen.github.io/xqtl-protocol/code/association_scan/quantile_models/qr_and_twas.html>

### Hierarchical multiple-testing (HMT)

Gene-level and variant-level FDR control across quantiles and contexts,
as used in the manuscript (per-gene screening → across-gene correction):

<https://statfungen.github.io/xqtl-protocol/code/association_scan/qtl_association_postprocessing.html>

### EOO enrichment

"Enrichment of overlapping" (EOO) analysis comparing qQTL-only vs. xQTL
variants against functional annotation categories (cell-type-specific
regulatory elements, chromatin interaction loops, TF binding sites,
etc.), as used to produce the enrichment figures in the manuscript:

<https://statfungen.github.io/xqtl-protocol/code/enrichment/eoo_enrichment.html>

### Stratified LD score regression (sLDSC)

Heritability partitioning across qQTL and xQTL annotations, with joint
modelling to quantify the additional trait heritability explained by
distributional effects beyond linear QTLs:

<https://statfungen.github.io/xqtl-protocol/code/enrichment/sldsc_enrichment.html>

### TWAS / cTWAS association testing

Applying qTWAS prediction weights (trained in `qQTLR`) to GWAS summary
statistics to produce gene–trait associations. This is where the actual
qTWAS association test lives, using the `pecotmr` / cTWAS integration:

<https://statfungen.github.io/xqtl-protocol/code/pecotmr_integration/twas_ctwas.html>

## When to use what

| Task                                                    | Where              |
|---------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|
| Interactive qQTL exploration on a single gene / region  | `qQTLR`            |
| QUAIL vQTL on a single phenotype                        | `qQTLR`            |
| Heterogeneity (ξ) assessment                            | `qQTLR`            |
| qTWAS **weight training** at scale                      | `qQTLR`            |
| Genome-wide qQTL calling with HMT                       | FunGen-xQTL pipeline |
| EOO / sLDSC enrichment                                  | FunGen-xQTL pipeline |
| Applying qTWAS weights to GWAS (association test)       | FunGen-xQTL pipeline |

## Citation

When using this software or the associated pipelines, please cite:

> Liu, Jiang, et al. *Distributional genetic effects reveal
> context-dependent molecular regulation in human brain aging and
> Alzheimer's disease.* 2026+ (manuscript in preparation,
> FunGen-xQTL consortium).

## See also

- [Basic qQTL analysis](basic-qqtl-analysis.html)
- [Interpreting qQTL results](interpreting-qqtl-results.html)
- [vQTL as a special case of qQTL](vqtl-special-case.html)
- [iQTL simulation settings](iqtl-simulation.html)
- [Training qTWAS prediction weights](qtwas-pipeline.html)
